text:
is a small nation occupying a part of the . 

Hizuru previously allied itself with the Eldian Empire, and is the only nation in the world willing to engage in diplomacy with Paradis, due to it wanting to monopolize the natural resources found on the island.

Geography

Not much is known about Hizuru's geography, but the nation is considered to be oriental, meaning that Hizuru is part of the eastern portion of the world. 

Politics

The leader of the nation of Hizuru is called "shogun", which means that the government is a shogunate. 

Even though the country suffered heavily from its former alliance with the Eldian Empire, it still has managed to establish itself in the international political stage in modern times. The Azumabito family, an influential clan descended from the former Shogun from a century ago, represent Hizuru as ambassadors to foreign nations. As an example, Kiyomi, the actual leader of the clan, personnally knew the globally celebrated Willy Tybur. 

Culture

Hizuru is known throughout the world for having been an ally of the Eldian Empire before the Great Titan War. Thanks to this alliance with the global superpower of that time, the country of Hizuru managed to prosper. After their defeat during that worldwide conflict, their reputation was tarnished for having been allies with the universally despised empire, and their nation fell from glory, being reduced to a small struggling oriental country. 

Due to this former alliance, Hizuru is unique from the world's nations in being the only one that tolerates Eldians, though it is unknown whether the country hosts a sizeable population of Subjects of Ymir or not. 

In recent times, Hizuru is known for producing talented engineers and developing relatively advanced weaponry and aircraft. Those engineers have been able to produce the first iceburst stone fueled flying boat capable of making intercontinental travel. 

According to Kiyomi, Hizuru has an exquisite seafood culture. 

History

Over a hundred years ago, Hizuru was allies with the Eldian Empire. A son from the Shogun clan (that would later become the Azumabito family) had a close relationship with the Fritz family and often visited Paradis Island. When King Karl Fritz moved the capital of Eldia to Paradis and erected the three Walls, that son was stranded on the island. The son who stayed in the island later began the Asian clan inside the Walls. After Hizuru's defeat during the Great Titan War and the Eldian Empire's collapse, its reputation was tarnished and the nation was thrown into disarray. 

Story

Marley Arc

Early in the Marley Mid-East War, Kiyomi Azumabito is invited by Zeke Jaeger to join forces with the residents of Paradis. After some back and forth discussions, Zeke is able to convince Kiyomi that the nation would greatly benefit due to the presence of Iceburst Stone on the island and they formally agree to join forces. Hizuru then begins to help Paradis prepare for an assault on Marley at some point in the future. 

In 852, Kiyomi visits Paradis Island as an ambassador for Hizuru. She meets Mikasa Ackermann, the descendant from the Shogun clan that had once held power in Hizuru. In a meeting, Kiyomi explains a method in which Hizuru would aid Paradis island in modernizing its infrastructure, although she inadvertently makes it clear that Hizuru is allying primarily for financial incentive.

In 853, Hizuru refuses to help Paradis Island trade with other nations because they want to keep all of its resources for themselves.After the end of the war, Willy Tybur calls upon the leaders of other countries to join him for a festival in Liberio where he would announce his solution to the problem of Titans. Hizuru sends Kiyomi and several others to attend the event. At a gathering on the eve of the festival, she expresses sympathy towards the Subjects of Ymir, by covering up for a Warrior candidate spilling wine over her kimono. Kiyomi then departs, leaving the candidates in awe of her benevolence.

War for Paradis arc

After the Raid on Liberio, Kiyomi visits Paradis with an observation unit to witness the Titans' rumbling and informs Dhalis Zachary the leaders of Hizuru have nothing but praise for the bravery shown by the nation of Eldia. Zachary thanks them while stating their victory would not be possible had it not been for the strong trust their nations have in one another. 

Trivia

The name is phonetically similar to , a name for which translates as "Land of the Rising Sun," referring to the nation's location in the far east of the early known world. In the context of Attack on Titan, this becomes ironic since the sun is said to rise in the west in this reality.
